core_mechanism: A nonlinear multiplicative cascade of a conserved quantity down a discrete self-similar hierarchy of coupled modes, whose anomalous scaling is organized by singular solutions — self-similar fronts propagating across scales that build up intermittency, and finite-complex-time singularities that stall the cascade — with a critical control-parameter threshold separating regular from chaotic dynamics.
brief_reason: The abstract frames the method around two generic singular fluctuations of the scale-cascade dynamics (self-similar solutions building intermittency and complex-time singularities inhibiting the cascade and promoting chaos) and a critical proportion of interactions marking the boundary between chaotic and regular dynamics.
